style: High pressing, possession-based with quick transitions. Emphasis on width from fullbacks, especially Trent Alexander-Arnold's playmaking from right-back. Strong counter-pressing after losing the ball.
weaknesses: Need long-term successor for Salah on right wing
tactical_notes: Under Slot, Liverpool transitioned to a more possession-dominant style compared to Klopp's heavy metal football. The system relies on patient build-up with sudden vertical passes to break lines. Fullbacks are crucial for width, and the number 10 role is key for linking midfield to attack.

style: Possession dominance, positional play. Inverted fullbacks tucking into midfield. Fluid attacking positions with emphasis on creating numerical superiority in key zones.
tactical_notes: Guardiola's system revolves around Rodri as the deep-lying playmaker. Without him, City lose their ability to control tempo. The inverted fullback system creates a 3-2-5 in possession. Haaland provides the direct goal threat that previous Guardiola teams lacked.

style: Structured possession with aggressive pressing. Set-piece specialists. Strong defensive organization with quick attacks through wide areas. Emphasis on left-side overloads.
weaknesses: Can be too rigid in attack
tactical_notes: Arteta has built a system around defensive solidity and set-piece threat. Saka is the primary creative outlet on the right, cutting inside on his left foot. Rice provides defensive security and box-to-box running. The team needs a clinical finisher to convert their chances.

style: Flexible attacking system with world-class individual talent. Can dominate possession or play on the counter. Bellingham as an advanced midfielder connecting play. Vinicius and Mbappé as twin attacking threats.
tactical_notes: Ancelotti faces the challenge of fitting Mbappé and Vinicius in the same system. The 4-3-1-2 or 4-4-2 diamond allows both to play centrally/wide. Bellingham's role is more advanced, acting as a de facto second striker at times. Valverde provides the engine and defensive cover on the right.

style: Classic Barça DNA with high defensive line and intense pressing. Youth development focus with La Masia graduates. Quick passing combinations through the middle with width from wingers.
tactical_notes: Under Flick, Barcelona plays an extremely high defensive line, sometimes pushing to the halfway line. This creates space in behind but is mitigated by intense pressing. Yamal at 17 is already a key creative force on the right. Pedri orchestrates from central midfield. The team depends heavily on Lewandowski for goals despite his age.

style: Dominant possession with aggressive pressing. Wide play through fullbacks with inverted wingers cutting inside. Kane as the focal point of attack with Musiala providing creativity.
tactical_notes: Under Kompany, Bayern aim to maintain their possession-heavy approach. Kane drops deep to link play, creating space for Musiala and the wingers to attack. The high defensive line can be exposed on transitions, which is an area for improvement.

style: Possession-based with patient build-up. Emphasis on collective play over individual brilliance post-Mbappé. High pressing and positional rotations in attack.
tactical_notes: Luis Enrique has transformed PSG from a star-dependent team to a more collective unit. Without Mbappé, the goals are spread more evenly. Dembélé provides width and directness on the right, while Kvaratskhelia adds flair on the left. The team still searches for a reliable 20+ goal striker.

style: Back three with wing-backs providing width. Emphasis on defensive structure and quick transitions. Building from the back with progressive passing through midfield.
tactical_notes: Amorim's 3-4-3 system is a significant tactical shift. The wing-backs are crucial for width, while the front three needs to press intensely. Bruno Fernandes operates as the creative hub, but the team needs more defensive solidity in midfield and defenders comfortable in a back three.

style: Possession-oriented with Guardiola influences. Emphasis on building from the back through the goalkeeper, inverted fullbacks in midfield, and creative overloads in wide areas.
weaknesses: Too many squad players creating selection issues
Lack of a proven elite striker
tactical_notes: Maresca implements a Guardiola-inspired positional play system. Cole Palmer is the creative heartbeat operating between the lines. Caicedo and Enzo Fernández form a double pivot, with one inverting fullback creating a midfield triangle. The massive squad investment means competition for places is fierce.

style: Aggressive, front-foot attacking football with a very high defensive line. Emphasis on dominating possession and relentless pressing. Full-backs push high to create width, inverted wingers cut inside.
tactical_notes: Postecoglou's non-negotiable high line and aggressive pressing creates an exciting but vulnerable system. Van de Ven's recovery pace is essential to cover the space behind the defense. Maddison operates as the creative number 10, while Son provides the goal threat from the left.

style: Direct, high-energy attacking play with emphasis on quick transitions and wide overloads. Strong defensive structure with aggressive pressing triggers. Physical, intense style suited to the Premier League.
tactical_notes: Howe builds attacks through wide areas with overlapping fullbacks and inverted wingers. Isak is the focal point up front with excellent movement and finishing. Bruno Guimarães controls tempo from midfield. Gordon provides direct running and goals from the left wing.

style: Intense pressing with quick vertical transitions. Compact defensive shape out of possession, rapid attacks through the flanks. Emery's trademark tactical discipline with aggressive ball recovery.
tactical_notes: Emery has transformed Villa into a pressing machine. Watkins' tireless running sets the press from the front. Rogers has emerged as a dynamic creative force in the number 10 role. Kamara anchors the midfield defensively, and Pau Torres provides composure in the build-up.

style: High-pressing, possession-oriented football with aggressive ball recovery. Quick combinations in the final third with emphasis on creating chances through central and wide overloads.
tactical_notes: Iraola's pressing system is among the most intense in the Premier League. The team plays well above their individual talent level through collective organization. Semenyo provides pace and directness on the right, while Kerkez offers dynamic runs from left-back.

style: Data-driven, possession-based football with emphasis on progressive passing and pressing. Fluid positional rotations in attack with players interchanging freely.
weaknesses: Losing key players to bigger clubs
tactical_notes: Brighton continue their identity of progressive, data-informed football. Mitoma's dribbling ability on the left is a primary attacking weapon. The team excels at developing young talents and selling at profit, making recruitment a competitive advantage.

style: Direct, set-piece focused with emphasis on crossing and aerial duels. Organized defensive block with quick transitions. Long throws as a tactical weapon.
weaknesses: Over-reliance on Mbeumo and Toney replacements
tactical_notes: Frank's Brentford is a set-piece machine, scoring a disproportionate number of goals from dead-ball situations. Mbeumo has stepped up as the primary attacking threat. The team uses a data-driven approach to recruitment, finding undervalued talent in lesser-known leagues.

style: Balanced, possession-oriented football with emphasis on build-up through midfield. Controlled pressing with quick transitions. Technical players throughout the squad.
weaknesses: Lack of cutting edge in attack
tactical_notes: Silva has built a well-organized, hard-to-beat side. Robinson is one of the best left-backs in the league with his overlapping runs. Pereira provides creativity from the number 10 position. Palhinha anchors the midfield with disciplined defensive work.

style: Structured build-up play with emphasis on wide areas. Patient possession with quick switches of play. More tactical discipline compared to Moyes era.
tactical_notes: Lopetegui brings a more possession-oriented approach to West Ham. Kudus provides unpredictable dribbling and creativity from wide or central positions. Bowen offers tireless running and goals from the right. Paquetá links midfield and attack with his technical quality.

style: Counter-attacking with a compact defensive block. Quick transitions through direct passes to pacey forwards. Strong Portuguese influence in recruitment and playing style.
weaknesses: Struggling to score from open play
tactical_notes: O'Neil sets up Wolves to be defensively compact and dangerous on the break. Cunha is the key creative outlet who drops deep to receive and drive forward. Neto provides blistering pace on the wing. The Portuguese pipeline through agent Jorge Mendes remains a key recruitment advantage.

style: Pragmatic, defensively solid football with emphasis on physicality and set pieces. Direct attacking play with early crosses into the box. Organized low block out of possession.
weaknesses: Squad quality gap to top half
tactical_notes: Dyche's Everton is built on defensive solidity and set-piece threat. Tarkowski organizes the backline. McNeil provides the creative spark from wide areas. The team's limited budget means they must maximize output from limited resources, relying on organization over individual talent.

style: Transitional play with emphasis on quick forward passes. Compact defensive shape with counter-attacking threat through pacey wingers.
weaknesses: Adapting back to Premier League level
tactical_notes: Leicester's return to the Premier League requires adjusting to the increased intensity. Vardy remains a potent threat on the counter despite his age. The team needs to balance defensive stability with enough attacking output to survive.

style: Compact defensive block with quick counter-attacks. Intense pressing in key zones. Physical, aggressive style with tactical discipline. Direct transitions through wide channels.
tactical_notes: Simeone's Atletico remains one of the hardest teams to break down in Europe. The 4-4-2 shape is compact and aggressive. Griezmann drops between the lines to create, while Sörloth provides the physical presence up front. De Paul's energy in midfield is crucial for their pressing game.

style: High-energy pressing with emphasis on physicality and intensity. Direct, aggressive attacking play with width from fullbacks. Basque-only recruitment policy shapes the squad identity.
tactical_notes: Valverde's Athletic is built on relentless energy and pressing. Nico Williams is one of La Liga's most exciting wingers with explosive pace and dribbling. Brother Iñaki provides tireless running and never misses a match. The Basque-only policy limits the transfer market but creates unmatched squad unity.

style: Possession-based, technically refined football. Patient build-up from the back with emphasis on midfield control. Quick combinations in the final third with runners from deep.
tactical_notes: Alguacil's Sociedad plays some of the most aesthetically pleasing football in La Liga. Zubimendi is the metronome in midfield, dictating tempo with his passing range. Kubo provides flair and creativity on the right. Oyarzabal leads the line with intelligent movement.

style: Pragmatic, results-oriented approach. Organized defensive structure with quick transitions. Europa League DNA with knockout-stage mentality.
tactical_notes: Sevilla is in a transitional period, trying to rebuild while maintaining their competitive European identity. The club's scouting network remains excellent at finding value signings. Defensive solidity is the foundation, with En-Nesyri providing the goal threat.

style: Direct, counter-attacking football with emphasis on quick transitions. Compact defensive shape with two banks of four. Physicality and pace in attack.
tactical_notes: Baraja works within severe financial constraints, relying on youth and team spirit. Mamardashvili is one of the best goalkeepers in La Liga. Duro leads the line with work rate and clever movement. The team punches above their weight through collective effort.

style: Technically proficient, possession-based with emphasis on quick passing combinations. Strong build-up play from the back with creative midfielders pulling strings.
tactical_notes: Villarreal's 'Yellow Submarine' identity revolves around technical football and smart recruitment. Baena has emerged as a creative force in midfield. Parejo provides experience and passing range. The team competes consistently above expectations relative to their market size.

style: Attacking, possession-heavy football with flair and creativity. Emphasis on through balls and combinative play in the final third. Entertaining style that prioritizes attack.
tactical_notes: Pellegrini's Betis is built around creative players who can unlock defenses. Isco and Fekir provide moments of magic in the final third. Lo Celso adds dynamism from midfield. The trade-off is defensive vulnerability, as the team commits heavily to attack.

style: Fast-paced, transition-oriented football. Emphasis on quick vertical play and exploiting space behind opposition defenses. Passionate attacking play at the Signal Iduna Park.
tactical_notes: Under Şahin, Dortmund aim to maintain their tradition of fast, attacking football. Brandt is the creative hub in the number 10 role. Adeyemi provides explosive pace on the wing. The team's ability to develop young players (Bellingham, Sancho previously) remains a key strength.

style: Dominant possession with a back three allowing fullbacks to push high. Controlled, patient build-up with sudden bursts of speed in transition. Extremely well-coached pressing structure.
tactical_notes: Xabi Alonso's Leverkusen was the sensation of 2023/24 with an unbeaten Bundesliga title. The 3-4-2-1 allows Grimaldo and Frimpong to bomb forward as wing-backs. Wirtz is the creative genius behind the striker. Xhaka's leadership and passing transformed the midfield.

style: Dynamic, high-pressing system with wingbacks providing width. Quick transitions and direct attacking play. Strong mentality and physicality.
weaknesses: Losing key players in transfer windows
tactical_notes: Toppmöller's Frankfurt plays an aggressive, high-energy game. Marmoush has emerged as one of the Bundesliga's most exciting attackers with pace and finishing. The 3-4-2-1 formation provides flexibility between defensive solidity and attacking width.

style: High-pressing, possession-oriented with emphasis on positional play and quick combinations. Aggressive defensive line with brave build-up from the back.
tactical_notes: Hoeneß has transformed Stuttgart into one of Germany's most exciting teams. The team plays with remarkable intensity and tactical discipline for their resources. Guirassy's goals are crucial, and Millot provides creative spark from midfield.

style: Compact, well-organized system with emphasis on quick transitions and collective effort. Wingbacks provide width in a 3-5-2 shape. Team-first mentality over individual brilliance.
tactical_notes: Werner's Bremen is a well-drilled unit that compensates for limited individual quality with organization and effort. Ducksch provides the goal threat with clever movement. Weiser drives forward from wing-back. The 3-5-2 gives them defensive solidity and midfield control.

style: Fast-paced, transition-oriented football with emphasis on quick forward passing. Pressing-focused approach with young, energetic players.
weaknesses: Lack of experience at the top level
tactical_notes: Seoane brings intensity and tactical structure to Gladbach. The team plays direct, vertical football that suits their athletic squad. Kleindienst has become a reliable goalscorer, and Koné's dynamism in midfield drives the team's pressing game.

style: Well-organized, disciplined football built on collective effort and smart recruitment. Compact defensive shape with purposeful attacking play.
tactical_notes: Freiburg's success under Streich continues with Schuster maintaining the club's identity of punching above their weight. Grifo provides creativity and set-piece expertise. The club's model of developing and maximizing players makes them perennial overachievers in the Bundesliga.

style: Tactically sophisticated 3-5-2 with dominant wing-play. Possession-based with patient build-up and devastating counter-attacks. Exceptional defensive structure with three center-backs covering all space.
tactical_notes: Inzaghi's Inter is the most well-oiled machine in Serie A. The 3-5-2 is executed to perfection — Dimarco and Dumfries provide width and crosses, Çalhanoğlu dictates from the regista role, and Barella's box-to-box energy drives the midfield. Lautaro and Thuram form one of Europe's best strike partnerships.

style: Possession-based build-up with emphasis on width through fullbacks. Quick combinations in the final third with creative attackers interchanging positions.
tactical_notes: Fonseca brings a more attacking philosophy to Milan. Leão's explosive pace and dribbling on the left is the primary attacking weapon. Pulisic has thrived in the number 10/right wing role with goals and assists. Theo Hernández remains one of the world's best attacking left-backs.

style: Possession-oriented with emphasis on controlled build-up and defensive discipline. Patient attacking play with emphasis on midfield superiority. Progressive from the back through short passing.
weaknesses: Lack of creativity in final third
tactical_notes: Motta brings a fresh approach to Juventus, emphasizing possession and progressive play over the traditional Italian defensive pragmatism. Vlahović is the focal point in attack but needs better service. The defense remains solid as part of Juve's DNA.

style: Defensively rock-solid with quick, clinical counter-attacks. Compact shape without the ball, explosive transitions with the ball. Conte's trademark intensity and winning mentality.
tactical_notes: Conte has immediately restored Napoli's defensive solidity. The 3-5-2 is compact and disciplined, with lightning-fast transitions through Kvaratskhelia's dribbling and Osimhen's movement. Lobotka controls tempo from deep midfield. Di Lorenzo provides leadership and attacking runs from right-back.

style: Aggressive man-marking system with relentless pressing. High-intensity, physically demanding football with players covering huge distances. Wing-backs essential for width, strikers interchange constantly.
weaknesses: Man-marking can be exploited by mobile attackers
tactical_notes: Gasperini's Atalanta is one of the most unique tactical systems in football. The aggressive man-marking approach requires extreme fitness and tactical intelligence. Lookman's Europa League final heroics cemented his status as the main man. De Ketelaere provides creative spark behind the strikers.

style: Attacking, progressive football with emphasis on vertical passing and quick transitions. Energetic pressing with a young, hungry squad.
tactical_notes: Baroni has energized Lazio with a more aggressive, pressing-oriented approach. The team plays with high intensity and directness. Zaccagni provides creativity and goals from the left wing, while the midfield engine drives the high press.

style: Passionate, attack-minded football built on strong defensive foundations. Quick transitions with direct passing to pacy attackers. Emotional connection between team and fans.
tactical_notes: De Rossi brings passion and tactical intelligence as a former Roma legend. Dybala remains the creative heartbeat when fit, capable of unlocking any defense. Lukaku provides the physical presence and goal threat. Pellegrini captains the side with all-around midfield play.

style: Attacking, aesthetically pleasing football with emphasis on passing combinations and creative freedom. Quick movement off the ball with players interchanging positions fluidly.
tactical_notes: Palladino has brought an attacking identity to Fiorentina. The team plays attractive football with emphasis on through-balls and movement. Conference League runs have given the squad European experience and confidence.

style: High-pressing, aggressive football with emphasis on winning the ball high up the pitch. Quick transitions into attack with direct, vertical passing. Energetic and physically demanding system.
tactical_notes: Bologna's surprise Champions League qualification under Motta set the stage for Italiano to continue the upward trajectory. The team's identity is built on collective effort and high-energy pressing rather than individual talent. Orsolini provides the creative spark from the right.

style: Attacking football with emphasis on technical quality and youth development. Quick passing combinations through midfield with creative players between the lines.
tactical_notes: Sage has stabilized Lyon after a turbulent period. Cherki is the jewel of their academy, providing moments of brilliance in the final third. Lacazette leads by example with his work rate and finishing. The club's financial situation limits their ability to compete in the transfer market.

style: Possession-dominant, build-up focused football with De Zerbi's trademark progressive passing from the back. Creative overloads in wide areas with emphasis on technical quality.
tactical_notes: De Zerbi brings his Brighton philosophy to Marseille — progressive passing from goalkeeper, inverted fullbacks, and creative freedom in the final third. The Vélodrome provides one of football's most intense atmospheres. The team needs time to fully absorb De Zerbi's complex positional play system.

style: Pragmatic, well-organized football with emphasis on defensive structure and counter-attacking. Strong development club that produces and sells top talent.
tactical_notes: Lille's business model revolves around developing and selling talent at profit (Hazard, Pépé, Osimhen, Sanches). David is one of Europe's most clinical strikers and likely to move to a bigger club. Yoro has emerged as one of the most promising young center-backs in world football.

style: Attacking, youth-focused football with emphasis on fast transitions and direct play. High pressing with energetic young players. Progressive recruitment strategy targeting young talent.
tactical_notes: Hütter's Monaco plays attractive, attacking football built around young talent. The tax-free principality gives them a recruitment edge for player wages. Ben Seghir has emerged as an exciting young prospect. Golovin provides creativity and experience in midfield.

style: Technical, possession-based football with strong youth academy pipeline. Emphasis on short passing and building from the back. Progressive and modern coaching approach.
tactical_notes: Rennes continues to punch above their weight through excellent youth development and smart recruitment. The academy has produced the likes of Camavinga and Dembélé. The team plays technical, attractive football that develops players for bigger clubs.

style: Ultra-aggressive high pressing with a very high defensive line. Fast, direct attacking play with emphasis on winning the ball back immediately after losing it. Extremely intense style requiring peak fitness.
tactical_notes: Schmidt's Benfica plays one of the most aggressive pressing systems in European football. The high line can be suicidal against pacy attackers but is devastatingly effective against most opponents. João Neves has emerged as one of Europe's most promising young midfielders.

style: Intense, combative football with strong defensive organization and powerful counter-attacks. Physical, aggressive approach with direct transitions. Champions League warriors mentality.
tactical_notes: Conceição's Porto is built on fight and intensity. The 4-4-2 is compact defensively with quick transitions through the pace of Galeno and the movement of Taremi. Porto's European knockout record under Conceição has been impressive, consistently punching above their weight.

style: Dominant possession with a back three. Wing-backs provide width while inside forwards cut in. Quick transitions through central areas with emphasis on interchanging attacking positions.
tactical_notes: Amorim's 3-4-3 has become Sporting's identity. Gyökeres has been a revelation with 40+ goal seasons, making him one of Europe's most wanted strikers. The system allows wing-backs to create width while the three forwards interchange fluidly. Hjulmand anchors the midfield with intelligence and passing.

style: Total Football heritage with emphasis on possession, youth development, and attacking play. Building from the back through technically gifted players. The Cruyff legacy of attractive, attacking football.
weaknesses: Eredivisie competitive gap to top leagues
tactical_notes: Ajax are in a rebuilding phase after losing many key players in recent windows. The Cruyff DNA of 4-3-3, youth development, and attractive football remains the foundation. Brobbey provides physical presence up front, while Taylor offers creativity from midfield.

style: Attacking, possession-dominant football with high pressing. Emphasis on fast, direct passing and creative overloads in the final third. Bosz's trademark aggressive, front-foot approach.
tactical_notes: Bosz's PSV plays the most attractive football in the Eredivisie. The team dominates domestically with overwhelming attacking quality. Simons' creative genius drives the team forward, while Veerman's passing range controls the tempo from midfield.

style: Structured, tactical football with emphasis on controlled pressing and quick transitions. Patient build-up with sudden bursts of attacking speed. Strong defensive organization.
tactical_notes: Slot built Feyenoord into a well-oiled tactical machine before his move to Liverpool. Giménez is one of the Eredivisie's most clinical finishers. The team's structure and organization regularly outperforms their individual talent level. De Kuip provides one of the most intense atmospheres in European football.

style: Compact, pragmatic football focused on defensive solidity and quick counter-attacks. Hardworking team that relies on collective effort and organization to compete in the Eredivisie.
tactical_notes: NAC Breda competes in the Eredivisie as a promoted side with limited resources. The team relies on organization, effort, and team spirit to compensate for individual quality gaps. Survival is the primary objective, built on defensive discipline and set-piece threat.
